title: (BUG) generate_id() produces non-monotonic IDs on NTP clock adjustment
description: id.rs generate_id() uses `SystemTime::now()` for the millisecond component. If the system clock is adjusted backwards (NTP sync, VM migration, leap second smear), `now_ms` can be less than the previously-stored `prev_ms`. The code takes the else branch (since now_ms != prev_ms), sets seq=0, and the CAS succeeds — producing an ID smaller than the previous one. Consequences: (1) IDs are no longer monotonically increasing, breaking pagination that relies on `id > ?` / `id < ?` ordering; (2) if the clock rewinds to a previously-used millisecond, the same (ms, seq=0) pair is reused, producing a duplicate ID that will violate PRIMARY KEY constraints. Fix: before accepting now_ms, clamp it to `max(now_ms, prev_ms)` to guarantee forward progress even when the wall clock goes backwards.
